findings.title: A Mechanistic Analysis of Looped Reasoning Language Models
findings.abstract: Reasoning has become a central capability in large language models. Recent research has shown that reasoning performance can be improved by looping an LLM's layers in the latent dimension, resulting in looped reasoning language models. Despite promising results, few works have investigated how their

findings.title: ClawGuard: A Runtime Security Framework for Tool-Augmented LLM Agents Against Indirect Prompt Injection
findings.abstract: Tool-augmented Large Language Model (LLM) agents have demonstrated impressive capabilities in automating complex, multi-step real-world tasks, yet remain vulnerable to indirect prompt injection. Adversaries exploit this weakness by embedding malicious instructions within tool-returned content, which

findings.title: HDR Video Generation via Latent Alignment with Logarithmic Encoding
findings.abstract: High dynamic range (HDR) imagery offers a rich and faithful representation of scene radiance, but remains challenging for generative models due to its mismatch with the bounded, perceptually compressed data on which these models are trained. A natural solution is to learn new representations for HDR

findings.title: General365: Benchmarking General Reasoning in Large Language Models Across Diverse and Challenging Tasks
findings.abstract: Contemporary large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated remarkable reasoning capabilities, particularly in specialized domains like mathematics and physics. findings.title: Towards Automated Pentesting with Large Language Models
findings.abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are redefining offensive cybersecurity by allowing the generation of harmful machine code with minimal human intervention. findings.title: Enhancing Program Repair with Specification Guidance and Intermediate Behavioral Signals
findings.abstract: Automated Program Repair (APR) has recently benefited from large language models (LLMs). However, most LLM-based APR approaches still rely primarily on coarse end-to-end signals from test-suite outcomes to guide repair, providing limited insight into where a program's internal logic deviates from it

findings.title: $λ_A$: A Typed Lambda Calculus for LLM Agent Composition
findings.abstract: Existing LLM agent frameworks lack formal semantics: there is no principled way to determine whether an agent configuration is well-formed or will terminate. findings.title: Discourse Diversity in Multi-Turn Empathic Dialogue
findings.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) produce responses rated as highly empathic in single-turn settings (Ayers et al., 2023; Lee et al., 2024), yet they are also known to be formulaic generators that reuse the same lexical patterns, syntactic templates, and discourse structures across tasks (Jiang et al., 2

findings.title: Collaborative Multi-Agent Scripts Generation for Enhancing Imperfect-Information Reasoning in Murder Mystery Games
findings.abstract: Vision-language models (VLMs) have shown impressive capabilities in perceptual tasks, yet they degrade in complex multi-hop reasoning under multiplayer game settings with imperfect and deceptive information. findings.title: Multi-ORFT: Stable Online Reinforcement Fine-Tuning for Multi-Agent Diffusion Planning in Cooperative Driving

findings.title: Ambivalence/Hesitancy Recognition in Videos for Personalized Digital Health Interventions

findings.title: Predicting User Satisfaction in Online Education Platforms: A Large Language Model Based Multi-Modal Review Mining Framework

findings.title: Evaluating Cooperation in LLM Social Groups through Elected Leadership

findings.title: Agentic Driving Coach: Robustness and Determinism of Agentic AI-Powered Human-in-the-Loop Cyber-Physical Systems
